finding: Control-plane hostname discovery, DNS resolution, HTTP reachability, served research identity, and exact deployed-commit verification are separate evidence layers and must not be collapsed into one deployment claim.
acceptance_rule: accepted=true only when retained evidence includes a DNS answer, completed HTTP response, verified research-surface identity, verified exact deployed commit, and no contradictions

verification.source_fetch: The committed module was fetched back successfully at test commit a1dfea1353be539340e6f32665dd16b700f5a5d2.
verification.runtime_test_reason: The automation runtime could not resolve github.com for a clean clone, and no GitHub Actions dispatch tool was available. No passing test result is claimed.

limits: The classifier evaluates retained observations; it does not itself perform DNS or HTTP requests.
A DNS answer can be stale or vantage-point-specific.
HTTP reachability does not identify content or source commit.
Served identity and commit verification do not establish scientific or medical truth.
falsification_route: Run node --test cloudflare-static/classify-hostname-resolution.test.mjs in a clean checkout.
Reject the artifact if DNS-only evidence can produce a served or deployed-commit claim.
Reject the artifact if exact-commit acceptance is possible without retained DNS, HTTP, and identity evidence.
